119059.explanation: Quality must come first. A good mix is desirable; gender should not affect quality.

119060.explanation: We have extensive participation. In addition, a municipal council that must make the final decision after hearing as many people as possible.

119063.explanation: We strive for livability in the centers which includes going out, but residents must also be able to live there comfortably.

119064.explanation: It will become unaffordable. Sustainable energy generation can also result in cost savings.

119066.explanation: If we continue to do it on a small scale.

119080.explanation: It's a pity this is necessary. We would rather be the first municipality free of enforcement officers.

119098.explanation: Where possible. This is not the municipality's responsibility.

119100.explanation: This is not a municipal task.

119104.explanation: Note: villages must not grow together. So sufficient buffer zones must be maintained.

119106.explanation: We must distribute rental homes evenly, treating all residents equally.

119107.explanation: Up to a maximum of, for example, 5 times.

119109.explanation: It is a nice facility but given the financial situation and sufficient swimming options in the municipality, now is not the time.

119111.explanation: Children often already have their diploma before school swimming starts.

119112.explanation: This is already provided for in the region.

119114.explanation: We will need to handle the scarce space and housing demand in Meierijstad differently.

119115.explanation: To meet the housing demand, we need to be more creative regarding the current housing stock.

119118.explanation: This is precisely where the strength of nature and the Vlagheide landscape park lies.

119119.explanation: Just like the cooperation with the North-East Brabant region.

119120.explanation: Current facilities must be maintained, and investments are necessary for that.

119122.explanation: Except when this is necessary for parking regulation.

119059.explanation: We do not want to enforce this. However, we commit ourselves to equal opportunities and a society where everyone, regardless of age, origin, belief, orientation, political preference, physical or mental disability, matters and can participate.

119060.explanation: We want to involve residents early in policy thinking. Initiatives such as citizen assemblies help bring residents and government closer together. A referendum, on the other hand, polarizes.

119063.explanation: With rules and frameworks, you provide clarity for visitors, operators, and residents.

119064.explanation: Residents are aware of the necessity of sustainability. The municipality supports residents' initiatives to achieve the set sustainability goals.

119066.explanation: In the coming years, the municipality must continue to contribute to sheltering people who have to flee. The current small-scale sheltering with active guidance to work is, for us, the most suitable form.

119071.explanation: Bulky waste can now be collected at home for a fee. We want to investigate whether free disposal at the recycling center is feasible in the future.

119073.explanation: We consider good accessibility important and want to encourage it.

119074.explanation: We believe that even people with a low income should be able to participate in sports in all villages of Meierijstad and therefore support the municipality's minimum income policy.

119080.explanation: Supervision and enforcement of rules are important. The already realized expansion of the capacity of the BOAs (neighborhood enforcement officers) provides sufficient space for this.

119083.explanation: Unfortunately, our BOAs are increasingly facing aggression. We focus on prevention and believe that good de-escalation training combined with bodycams can be effective.

119087.explanation: As a party, we have actively worked on this recently and want to maintain it to counter speculation.

119090.explanation: We find it important that the municipality focuses on paid employment, volunteer work, informal care, and meaningful daytime activities.

119094.explanation: We would like this to happen in consultation with residents and users. Directives from the municipality are not part of this.

119095.explanation: The current traffic rules are sufficient.

119098.explanation: Freedom of choice in healthcare is a right. However, we must realize that there is scarcity in healthcare and the luxury to choose is not always available.

119100.explanation: For people who cannot afford it themselves, we want to combat so-called period poverty through Menstruation Distribution Points.

119104.explanation: We find it important that the municipality makes clear agreements about the amount of industrial land available, so that economic growth fits the space and livability of Meierijstad. Growth for the sake of growth is not our goal; we want to facilitate local entrepreneurs.

119106.explanation: We find it important that the municipality provides housing for refugees who have received a residence permit (status holders).

119107.explanation: We support free waste disposal at the recycling center and want to investigate how this can be made possible.

119109.explanation: We have worked hard for the preservation of the outdoor pool in Veghel and are proud of that.

119111.explanation: The municipality currently does not enable school swimming; this is going well.

119112.explanation: We are in favor of 'denormalizing' drug use; a coffee shop does not belong in this.

119114.explanation: From an urban planning perspective, we must dare to build upwards in places where we currently do not.

119115.explanation: This is now possible under the leadership of our municipal government, and we would like to maintain it.

119118.explanation: We want to further develop the Vlagheide in Eerde as a recreational area, maintaining and strengthening nature and without expanding hospitality.

119119.explanation: We see cooperation as a means, not an end. The focus is on preserving our own identity and strength, with regional cooperation supported as long as it does not harm local interests.

119120.explanation: This implies that we are not doing this now; for us, all communities are equal, as also described in the future vision.

119122.explanation: To solve the parking issues in Veghel center, a paid parking garage is necessary.

119059.explanation: Lokaal Meierijstad finds it important that women are also present in the council. Lokaal Meierijstad believes it should not be mandatory to equalize the number of men and women. They prioritize quality in the new council over gender.

119060.explanation: Lokaal Meierijstad believes in a representative democracy where residents choose their representation. Residents' views on important issues are never black and white, and this nuance is not captured by a referendum.

119063.explanation: Lokaal Meierijstad finds it important that cafes and restaurants have extended opening hours so that people can make optimal use of these facilities. However, a closing time is desirable to limit nuisance to the neighborhood.

119064.explanation: There are national subsidy schemes that residents can apply for. Within the municipality of Meierijstad, there is a sustainability fund through which residents can borrow money on favorable terms for sustainability measures.

119066.explanation: The municipality of Meierijstad must comply with the Distribution Act. Closing the asylum reception center is (currently) not possible. Local Meierijstad supports small-scale, distributed reception of asylum seekers and opposes large-scale reception centers (AZC).

119071.explanation: Livability and a clean municipality are important to LOKAAL. By collecting bulky waste free of charge at people's homes, we prevent illegal dumping and environmental pollution. This is fair and accessible to everyone, including residents who are less mobile. This way, Meierijstad remains clean and livable.

119073.explanation: Local Meierijstad supports an inclusive society so that everyone can participate. Entrepreneurs themselves must ensure that their businesses are accessible to everyone, just as the municipality does for its buildings. There are national subsidy schemes available for entrepreneurs for this purpose.

119074.explanation: Local Meierijstad supports helping people with low incomes so that children can participate in sports. LOKAAL believes that sports should be accessible to everyone. For those who cannot afford it, financial support is available from the municipality.

119080.explanation: Local Meierijstad places great importance on the safety of our residents. The tasks of the community enforcement officers (BOAs) are increasing, so expansion is desired. BOAs should be more visible to give residents a sense of security.

119083.explanation: Local Meierijstad believes that BOAs should be allowed to carry a baton under certain conditions. BOAs performing tasks that involve risks should be able to carry a baton to ensure their own safety.

119087.explanation: The housing market in Meierijstad is under pressure. Houses are primarily meant to live in, not for investment. A self-occupancy obligation promotes social cohesion, safety in neighborhoods, and community spirit. However, LOKAAL remains mindful of exceptions, such as divorces and temporary work.

119090.explanation: Local Meierijstad considers participation in society according to the abilities of residents important. Everyone can contribute something to society.

119094.explanation: In the future, there will be more electric cars, and for this, electric charging points are needed. LOKAAL wants to include this in future parking policies but currently does not see an urgent need.

119095.explanation: The 30 km/h speed limit applies to residential areas and shopping centers. Through roads remain at 50 km/h. LOKAAL currently sees no added value in lowering the speed limit on all roads.

119098.explanation: People should be able to choose care that aligns with their religion, culture, or belief as much as possible. The municipality actively promotes diversity in care offerings, as long as the quality, accessibility, and affordability of care are guaranteed.

119100.explanation: Local Meierijstad values hygiene among residents. There is national policy on this. These resources are already offered at various locations within the municipality, such as youth centers.

119104.explanation: Local Meierijstad wants to enable local businesses to grow. Attention must be given to livability and a healthy living environment. Without prosperity, there is no well-being.

119106.explanation: Local Meierijstad opposes giving priority to refugees for social rental housing.

119107.explanation: Waste must be able to be disposed of free of charge at the recycling center. However, payment is required for residual waste. This prevents littering and illegal dumping in recreational areas.

119109.explanation: Local Meierijstad has fought hard to preserve the outdoor swimming pool. LOKAAL supports retaining existing facilities.

119111.explanation: In a country with much water, it is vital that all children learn to swim. LOKAAL supports physical education and the preservation of swimming safety among children. LOKAAL chooses safety, equal opportunities, and a practical approach that offers equal (swimming) chances for every schoolchild.

119112.explanation: According to Local Meierijstad, no coffee shop should be established in the municipality. Surrounding municipalities already have them, and Local Meierijstad wants to prevent any nuisance.

119114.explanation: Local Meierijstad believes that only certain locations are suitable for high residential flats. In most cores of Meierijstad, this is undesirable.

119115.explanation: Local Meierijstad welcomes creative solutions to combat the housing shortage. Renting out rooms is one of them.

119118.explanation: Without nature, there is no place for recreation. At the Vlagheide, nature and recreation can coexist. It is a beautiful area to enjoy nature.

119119.explanation: Local Meierijstad sees opportunities in cooperation with Brainport Eindhoven in mobility, knowledge exchange, and economy. We want to retain our own authority. The needs of our own residents remain a priority in this cooperation.

119120.explanation: Local Meierijstad advocates for a fair distribution of investments across all 13 population centers. Not only in Schijndel, but every core receives attention. Development where needed, always with consideration for the entire municipality, so everyone can benefit and Meierijstad remains strong as a whole.

119122.explanation: Local Meierijstad supports free parking. This increases the attractiveness of the shopping center. We want to maintain the blue zone in Veghel.

119059.explanation: Choose administrators based on quality; diversity is important, but no quotas.

119060.explanation: Prefer strong participation and good consultation; an advisory referendum might oversimplify possibly complex dossiers.

119063.explanation: Entrepreneurial space, provided nuisance and safety are well regulated and enforced.

119064.explanation: Facilitate and co-finance where effective (loan/subsidy), focusing on resident collectives and support.

119066.explanation: Meierijstad must not become a "magnetic" reception municipality; safety, support, and limits come first.

119071.explanation: Is expensive and counters dumping; requires tailored solutions/arrangements.

119073.explanation: Accessibility means participation; support should be targeted and temporary (e.g., co-financing) instead of open-ended.

119074.explanation: Sports prevent healthcare costs and isolation; through clear minimum schemes and easy applications.

119080.explanation: Visibility and tackling nuisance increase the sense of safety.

119083.explanation: If working in risk situations: good training, protocols, and proportionality.

119087.explanation: Against abuse and buyouts; homes are meant to be lived in.

119090.explanation: Reciprocity and participation, with tailored solutions (health/means) and guidance to work.

119094.explanation: Looking ahead: proper distribution, no uncontrolled growth, and consideration of parking pressure.

119095.explanation: Traffic safety yes, but with tailored measures: 30 km/h where necessary, traffic flow where possible.

119098.explanation: Freedom of choice and appropriate care, as long as quality and availability are guaranteed.

119100.explanation: Help with period poverty, but targeted through schools/minimum schemes rather than general distribution.

119104.explanation: Good for jobs and extra attention to the (local) business climate.

119106.explanation: Fair distribution: Give initial priority to local housing seekers and urgency based on need, they do not automatically take precedence.

119107.explanation: Create a low threshold at the outset.

119109.explanation: VVD has fought for a long time to preserve this; this is about livability, sports, and pride (citizens' initiative).

119111.explanation: Swimming safety is fundamental; preventing drownings is priceless.

119114.explanation: Differentiated high-rise buildings can be on a few logical locations, but Meierijstad remains village-like: quality and integration over “height for the sake of height.”

119115.explanation: Protect family homes and livability; only very limited and strictly regulated where it is truly necessary.

119118.explanation: Not 'either-or' but 'both-and': zoning, protecting nature, and enabling recreation.

119119.explanation: This fits our approach: connection with MRE/Brainport for jobs, talent development, accessibility, and opportunities for our youth, entrepreneurs, and all residents. For the VVD, and especially in this dossier for years: alone you run faster, together you get further.

119120.explanation: We invest evenly across all centers, including Schijndel, based on tasks and benefits for residents. Investing is giving substance to the future.

119122.explanation: Good for accessibility and the local economy; combine with smart rotation (blue zone) where needed.

119059.explanation: The best woman or man in the right place.

119060.explanation: A binding and corrective referendum at national, provincial, and municipal levels is a spearhead of our party. As long as this law is not yet passed, an advisory referendum with minimal barriers must be possible in our municipality.

119063.explanation: Additional rules should be set in connection with nuisance and public order.

119064.explanation: Energy poverty among tenants requires accelerated housing insulation and sustainability, especially in social rental housing and private rentals. Until then, financial support and substantive assistance to these residents are necessary.

119066.explanation: Our municipality has chosen for dispersed sheltering in consultation with COA.

119071.explanation: There are enough recycling centers in our municipality and at a low rate.

119073.explanation: Entrepreneurs are responsible for this themselves.

119074.explanation: This is part of the poverty policy. Everyone must be able to participate.

119080.explanation: Increase the visibility of the neighborhood police officer and BOAs in neighborhoods and districts.

119083.explanation: Ensure that police and BOAs can optimally continue their work. They must be able to defend themselves in threatening situations.

119087.explanation: Houses are for living in. Self-occupancy obligation in the fight against slumlords. So buying = living will be the slogan. This must be strictly enforced.

119090.explanation: It is important that people on social assistance, if personal circumstances permit, receive sustainable guidance to work with individual attention and training, in cooperation with employers and with good working conditions.

119094.explanation: The municipality must ensure sufficient charging stations, but this should not be just a profit model for operators. Active enforcement of correct use of the charging points: charging only, no long-term parking.

119095.explanation: We must encourage people to use access roads to leave (or enter) their neighborhoods. If you make access roads unattractive, people will more often choose alternatives where you actually want as little traffic as possible (through residential areas).

119098.explanation: In a time when so much is already asked of (and the number of) caregivers, this is generally, unfortunately, not feasible. Tailored solutions in extreme cases must remain possible.

119100.explanation: This would mean that people who can currently pay easily can also make use of it. For low-income groups, there are support arrangements.

119104.explanation: First infill development. Industrial areas should only be expanded if it brings employment for our own residents. Give space to our own small and medium-sized enterprises. They must be able to develop independently, regardless of the scale enlargement of the Metropolis / Brainport Eindhoven.

119106.explanation: Attention to all target groups in housing allocation and appropriate housing for vulnerable groups and the elderly.

119107.explanation: Everyone must be able to deliver their waste affordably and easily at the recycling centers in all major centers. Small amounts of bulky or household waste should not be a financial barrier. Proper waste separation must be encouraged. Affordability increases use of recycling centers and reduces waste.

119109.explanation: This concerns a swimming pool that is privately owned. So it is not even our own yet. It is irresponsible to handle public money this way.

119111.explanation: School swimming must remain a fixed part of primary education so that every child learns swimming skills and is swim-safe.

119112.explanation: A government that runs a major campaign against smoking while at the same time allowing a location where “smoking” is permitted would seem contradictory to us.

119114.explanation: No urbanization in our rural municipality.

119115.explanation: Possibilities such as splitting homes or converting business premises into residential units must be expanded and promoted.

119119.explanation: We watch with dismay as Meierijstad positions itself as an implementation office of Brainport, with Veghel as the shining center. Cooperation is good, but never at the expense of our residents. Their interests—affordable housing, livability, and manageable traffic pressure—must always come first.

119120.explanation: Just like in all other centers.

119122.explanation: No extra barriers to the accessibility of our retailers.

119059.explanation: Otherwise, the qualities of half of society are lost. Gemeentebelang will address this through the composition of the candidate list.

119060.explanation: Direct influence improves trust in governance and politics. This happens outside of parties. Current participation processes are often directed by direct stakeholders. Participants are often directly interested themselves. For the general interest, post-verification by all residents is important.

119063.explanation: Nuisance to the surroundings must be prevented. Visitors should be able to work again in the morning and contribute to society.

119064.explanation: In the context of permit issuance. It must remain small-scale. No large wind turbines or solar parks on agricultural land.

119066.explanation: Support for immigration is reaching its end. The municipality has a quota. Limiting migration is a matter for The Hague.

119071.explanation: Yes, many people do not have a car or their own transportation.

119073.explanation: It must be accessible to everyone. This can also be regulated through permit conditions.

119074.explanation: Sports should be possible for everyone.

119080.explanation: There is too little supervision, especially in rural areas.

119087.explanation: Housing is a fundamental right. Housing must not be a profit model for speculators.

119090.explanation: Depending on the possibilities of that person, if they have those possibilities, the Participation Act already tests this. Those who can work do not receive unemployment benefits.

119094.explanation: Vattenfall has already taken far too many parking spaces which are now unused all day.

119095.explanation: For safety, this is necessary. Roads must be safe for youth and the elderly.

119098.explanation: Society must offer good care. No new pillar system should be established.

119100.explanation: For people living at social assistance level.

119104.explanation: Especially for local businesses. Not for large box stores.

119106.explanation: Equal treatment with Dutch nationals. Possibly a temporary residence for 2 years to give them the opportunity to find something on their own.

119109.explanation: The outdoor pool must remain. It should become municipal property again and be renovated. Otherwise, it is capital destruction.

119111.explanation: All children must learn to swim. Currently, some parents drop out because of the costs.

119112.explanation: Alcohol and smoking already cause enough problems. No legalization of other problems.

119114.explanation: Housing research shows that residents prefer family homes.

119115.explanation: They must be independent homes with their own cooking facilities, toilet, and wash/shower facilities.

119118.explanation: Vlagheideberg for recreation; Eerdse bergen should maintain their nature function.

119119.explanation: Brainport is the economic engine of the region. Better connections are needed. Public transport. Bicycle connections. Meierijstad is oriented towards Eindhoven.

119120.explanation: Schijndel is a larger center where no construction has taken place for years.

119059.explanation: A municipal executive makes decisions for everyone. Therefore, it should have a good mix of men and women. Equal distribution provides more diverse experiences at the table and better decisions. Quality and diversity go well together.

119060.explanation: Residents should be able to participate in major decisions. A referendum can help if the rules are clear and the process is fair. It is a supplement to the council, not a replacement. This way, we make decisions together.

119063.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve space, but residents also deserve peace. Therefore, extended opening hours where possible, tailor-made solutions, and clear rules against nuisance. The municipality must be able to intervene in case of complaints. This keeps it pleasant and livable.

119064.explanation: Local energy from residents is good for the climate and the wallet. With municipal support, more people can participate, also those with a small budget. This way we keep profits in Meierijstad and become less dependent on expensive energy.

119066.explanation: We arrange shelter properly, safely, and temporarily where needed, with good guidance and clear agreements with the neighborhood and police. We stand for distribution, support, and no nuisance. Humane and well organized.

119071.explanation: Pickup can help prevent dumping and littering, but 'always free' makes it expensive for everyone. We want a basic arrangement that is affordable and extra help for those who really need it.

119073.explanation: Everyone must be able to enter a shop, café, or office. Accessibility is often a barrier, also due to costs. With a simple subsidy scheme, we help entrepreneurs with entrances, automatic doors, and toilets. That is fair and good for customers.

119074.explanation: Sports promote health and participation. Money should not be a barrier. Therefore, we reimburse fees through the Participation Scheme and for children via the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture. This way, everyone in Meierijstad can play sports.

119080.explanation: Safety is important. We deploy enforcement officers targeted where there is nuisance and work with the police. Social Work Meierijstad is also needed: youth work and help prevent problems. Additional officers only if smarter working is not enough.

119083.explanation: Enforcement officers are mainly there for supervision and addressing issues, not for fighting. A baton makes situations harder faster and can increase risks. We choose good training, communication, bodycams, and quick police support in danger.

119087.explanation: Houses are for living in, not for speculation. With a self-occupancy obligation, we keep owner-occupied homes available for starters and families and curb investors. This helps affordability and livability in neighborhoods.

119090.explanation: Participation is good, but it must fit someone's health and situation. We help people towards work, education, or volunteer work with guidance and trust. No compulsory 'penalty tasks,' but opportunities to gain experience and progress.

119094.explanation: Electric driving is becoming increasingly common. Therefore, enough charging points are needed, also in neighborhoods with few own driveways and at larger apartment complexes. We expand charging points smartly: well distributed, with space for shared cars, and not at the expense of green areas.

119095.explanation: 30 km/h makes streets safer and quieter, especially near schools and in residential areas. But not every main road is suitable. We choose 30 as the standard in neighborhoods and customize on busy routes with safe design and good traffic flow.

119098.explanation: Care works better when someone feels understood and safe. Therefore, where possible, there should be choice for care that fits faith or culture. However, within the law and with good quality. No one may be excluded or receive less care.

119100.explanation: Menstrual products are not a luxury. For some girls and women, they are too expensive, which is unfair. With free tampons and pads in schools, community centers, and the town hall, we tackle period poverty. Small gesture, big impact.

119104.explanation: Work is important, but space is scarce. First, better use of existing sites and vacancies. Expansion is only possible if really necessary and if it has clear added value for Meierijstad: good jobs, little nuisance, sustainable and well accessible.

119106.explanation: Everyone has the right to a fair chance at housing. Priority based on status is not fair in our view. However, status holders must be able to start quickly, through good guidance and temporary housing where possible. The solution is simple: more affordable houses for everyone.

119107.explanation: We want less litter on the streets and no dumping in forests, but more reuse. Many waste streams can be free or cheaper, but 'everything free' can lead to abuse and higher costs. Therefore: fair pricing, rewarding separation, and extra help for those in tight financial situations.

119109.explanation: Stopping support likely means closure. The outdoor pool is important for affordable swimming and social meeting. The council previously chose to keep it open. We continue to contribute, but only with a feasible plan: lower costs, make sustainable, fair contribution from operator/users.

119111.explanation: Swimming is a basic skill. School swimming reduces differences between children and prevents drowning. Therefore, the municipality will continue supporting this, especially for children with fewer opportunities at home. Safety comes first.

119112.explanation: Blocking does not help; it moves trade to the streets. Better: one or more coffeeshops with strict rules. Security must also monitor outside and address parking pressure. With good locations, agreements with the neighborhood, and enforcement, you prevent nuisance.

119114.explanation: We need housing, including upwards. But tall apartment blocks do not fit everywhere. We choose to build in the right places: near the center, public transport, and facilities, with green and parking well arranged.

119115.explanation: Room rental can help against housing shortage, especially for young people and starters. But it can also cause nuisance and excessive rents. Therefore: allow it with clear rules on safety, management, maximum numbers per street, and good enforcement.

119118.explanation: De Vlagheide is valuable precisely because of nature and silence. Therefore: nature first. Recreation is allowed but on a small scale and nature-friendly (walking, cycling) and with quiet in the evenings.

119119.explanation: Cooperating with Brainport can bring jobs and innovation. But not blindly going 'all in'. We choose projects that really help Meierijstad: good jobs, internships, sustainable industry, accessibility, and affordable housing, without extra pressure on landscape and livability.

119120.explanation: We invest in all cores, including Schijndel, based on what is needed: housing, accessibility, and facilities. Not 'more for the sake of more', but fair distribution and clear choices. This keeps Meierijstad balanced and makes each core feel seen.

119122.explanation: In the center, we want to keep parking affordable, but not always free everywhere. Short-term parking can be free or cheap. Long-term parking preferably at the edge. This keeps the center accessible and prevents search traffic and nuisance.

119059.explanation: We still have a long way to go in Meierijstad regarding female aldermen. So come on!

119060.explanation: With referenda, all nuance is lost. For complex issues, only yes or no can be answered. We opt for a citizen's council.

119063.explanation: This can lead to nuisance deep into the night and causes unwanted pressure on entrepreneurs and employees who do not want that.

119064.explanation: Of course they deserve support from the municipality. But financial support depends on the capacity of the residents who have the initiative.

119066.explanation: Thanks to the small scale of our shelter locations, there is no nuisance or other trouble. On the contrary: many asylum seekers are working in our municipality.

119071.explanation: But of course nothing is free, so the costs are naturally included in the waste disposal levy.

119073.explanation: All buildings, whether municipal, shops, businesses or others, must be naturally accessible to everyone. And in this, everyone has their own responsibility.

119080.explanation: We had a shortage and have already scaled up. If it still turns out to be insufficient, we will hear about it.

119083.explanation: If it is demonstrated that there is a need/requirement.

119087.explanation: Also here applies: it is already arranged. We have a self-occupancy obligation.

119090.explanation: That counter-performance is actually work, and that should simply be paid. We encourage people on benefits mainly to become active again and that is very good and works well.

119094.explanation: There is considerable parking pressure in several places. If it turns out there are too few parking spaces for electric cars, it will surely be addressed.

119095.explanation: That is already the case almost everywhere.

119098.explanation: Given the current labor market, unfortunately we cannot always afford that luxury.

119100.explanation: But, the municipality mainly ensures that people's incomes meet the needs. So that this symptom treatment is not necessary. If it is needed, we provide for it.

119104.explanation: We already have far too many industrial estates.

119106.explanation: They are urgent, but not more urgent than others with an urgency declaration.

119107.explanation: Because nothing is free. So the waste disposal levy will rise as a result. And people with little waste pay for the regular customers of the recycling center.

119109.explanation: Unfortunately, it will be a municipal swimming pool for the next 10 years. We voted against it. Four municipal pools in such a small municipality is really ridiculous.

119111.explanation: Why stay? We are not doing that now, you know.

119114.explanation: In Veghel, because Veghel has the task to urbanize.

119115.explanation: But then under the flag of projects like Onder de Pannen or Kamers met Aandacht. So no unbridled rental exploitation.

119118.explanation: From the start of the developments, we have always advocated for nature over recreation. And if there is recreation, it should be mainly freely accessible.

119119.explanation: This cooperation brings Meierijstad nothing but inconvenience. We are simply a good neighbor, just as we are to Den Bosch.

119122.explanation: Urban parking fees belong with Veghel's urban aspirations.

119059.explanation: D66 supports a more diverse executive board in terms of gender, origin, etc. The board does not necessarily have to have an equal number of men and women. However, suitable female candidates must be actively sought. When candidates have equal qualifications, preference should be given to the woman.

119060.explanation: Residents of Meierijstad can express their opinions in many ways, such as through consultation, participation, and contact with the council. An advisory referendum is not a suitable instrument for this. D66 does support more influence through a corrective referendum, which should be regulated nationally by law.

119063.explanation: Opening hours of cafes and restaurants affect not only entrepreneurs and visitors, but also residents and enforcement. Therefore, closing times must be determined in good consultation. They cannot be decided by hospitality entrepreneurs alone.

119064.explanation: Energy communities deserve space and support. By jointly generating and sharing sustainable energy locally, residents strengthen their own control, reduce energy bills, and work together for a sustainable future for Meierijstad.

119066.explanation: Everyone has a place in Meierijstad. The shelter here is small-scale, humane, and self-managed. Closure helps no one. Precisely with good shelter and active support towards work and participation, we ensure that people become part of our society as quickly as possible.

119071.explanation: D66 chooses the polluter pays principle. Free bulky waste collection sounds attractive but means higher costs for everyone. By placing costs on the user, we encourage reuse and less waste while keeping municipal charges fair and future-proof.

119073.explanation: Accessibility is important and necessary. The municipality must require entrepreneurs to make their buildings accessible and help those who cannot achieve this themselves (financially).

119074.explanation: Children from families receiving social assistance should receive reimbursement for sports club membership. Playing sports should be self-evident for them. For adults, support can be arranged through special social assistance depending on the situation.

119080.explanation: Enforcement is an essential part of improving safety in Meierijstad. Additional community officers provide more street visibility, quicker reporting, and a more pleasant and safer feeling in public spaces.

119083.explanation: Currently, there is no reason to do this in Meierijstad. The presence or use of weapons increases the risk of aggression and escalation.

119087.explanation: D66 Meierijstad supports the self-occupancy requirement for new-build homes, as already applies in Meierijstad. This helps to combat speculation. This obligation applies only to new homes. For existing homes, private sellers are free to sell under their own conditions.

119090.explanation: D66 Meierijstad wants to encourage people on social assistance to work, but not through mandatory counter-performances. Socially useful work should simply be paid and may not be a condition for receiving benefits.

119094.explanation: Electric driving helps to make Meierijstad cleaner and more sustainable and should be stimulated. Therefore, D66 wants more charging points and the deployment of smart charging stations so charging capacity is used efficiently and the power grid is not unnecessarily burdened.

119095.explanation: D66 Meierijstad supports 30 km/h zones in centers and residential streets. For access roads, this is less suitable. A general speed reduction is difficult to enforce and does not always contribute to traffic safety.

119098.explanation: D66 Meierijstad stands for freedom of choice in healthcare. Everyone must be able to receive care that fits their background. At the same time, this may never lead to refusal of care or caregivers based on religion, culture, or beliefs.

119100.explanation: Menstrual products are not a luxury. No one should miss school, sports, or other activities because they cannot afford these products. D66 Meierijstad therefore wants to make them freely available at schools and community facilities to prevent period poverty.

119104.explanation: Business parks must be able to expand so companies can grow and establish themselves in Meierijstad. According to D66, this can only be done under conditions, with sustainable construction and smart, multiple use of space.

119106.explanation: Everyone deserves a place in Meierijstad. The municipality has a national quota for housing status holders. Whether or not there is priority, this task remains. D66 therefore finds it logical to continue giving this priority.

119107.explanation: D66 disagrees. We adhere to the polluter pays principle. Recycling centers already offer many free waste streams. Free bulky waste collection sounds attractive but leads to higher costs for everyone. By placing costs on the user, we encourage less waste and reuse.

119109.explanation: D66 has fought hard to keep the outdoor pool in Veghel open. Stopping municipal support now is inexplicable. After renovation, the pool deserves stability and trust so that the initiative group and Sportfondsen can develop the pool further in the coming years.

119111.explanation: School swimming gives all children the chance to obtain a swimming diploma. This increases safety and helps prevent drownings, which unfortunately still happen dozens of times a year in the Netherlands.

119112.explanation: Cannabis use is legal in the Netherlands. It is therefore not logical to block the establishment of a coffeeshop outright. However, the municipality must closely monitor safety and the prevention of undermining and criminal activities.

119114.explanation: Space for housing is scarce. D66 Meierijstad sees appropriate high-rise buildings at suitable locations as an opportunity to build more homes without harming the open countryside. This strengthens vibrant and sustainable living environments, especially in Veghel and Schijndel, provided spatial considerations are carefully weighed.

119115.explanation: To address the housing shortage, we must use all appropriate means. Renting out rooms can contribute to this and is also seen by housing corporations as an effective tool to quickly provide more people with housing.

119118.explanation: D66 Meierijstad believes that nature and recreation on the Vlagheide must go together. Precisely by giving people space to recreate here, they can enjoy nature and the area becomes accessible and experiential.

119119.explanation: Cooperation with Brainport Eindhoven offers great opportunities for Meierijstad. Brainport is an engine of innovation and employment. By fully committing here, Meierijstad can better connect to developments in mobility, housing, and education, building a strong and future-oriented economy.

119120.explanation: Investment in Schijndel is necessary. By investing in an attractive center with space for meeting and art, less traffic through the center, and smart densification with appropriate high-rise buildings, Schijndel remains a livable and strong core, now and in the future.

119122.explanation: It must remain possible to park for free in the center of Veghel. This does not mean that all parking spaces must be free. This keeps the center accessible and creates room for new center plans for Veghel.

119059.explanation: We are against discrimination based on gender. We also believe that positions should be held by the most suitable person.

119060.explanation: In a democracy, it is essential that residents can have their voices heard on important topics, for example about the arrival of a refugee center.

119063.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves know best when their customers come.

119064.explanation: The municipality should not spend public money on the green dream of a few residents.

119066.explanation: Stop mass immigration, close the borders. Only then can we clearly see how many homes we lack and build a house for everyone. Otherwise, it is like mopping with the tap open.

119071.explanation: But if residents take the trouble to bring it to the municipal site, it should also be possible free of charge. This is to prevent illegal dumping.

119073.explanation: Here, an entrepreneur must take care of this themselves.

119080.explanation: Prefer more police, not more community officers. Public order is a matter for the police. We want to prevent powers from becoming diluted and slowly ending up in a police/surveillance state.

119083.explanation: The monopoly on violence and weapon use must lie with the police. That is why we also support having more “blue” presence on the streets.

119090.explanation: To find your way back into society, getting to work and contributing to the community is the best thing you can do to prevent isolation and move towards self-reliance.

119095.explanation: Making the entire built-up area low-traffic is not in the interest of the residents of Meierijstad, who would see their commuting times to and from work increase. Smooth traffic flow should be the priority, rather than slowing down and limiting traffic.

119100.explanation: This is not a shared task for the municipality.

119104.explanation: Commercial activity is good for local employment and the economy, but the benefits must outweigh the burdens. Moreover, careful consideration must be given to the traffic consequences.

119106.explanation: Social rental housing is intended for our own residents who have their roots in Meierijstad and sometimes wait up to 15 years on the list.

119107.explanation: It must be ensured that this operates at cost price and certainly should not become a cash cow for the municipality. If waste is dumped, costs for the community will increase even further.

119109.explanation: A suitable plan must be developed for the operation of this facility that makes a positive contribution to the livability of our municipality.

119112.explanation: Preventing legal, controlled coffeeshops only fuels the illegal trade. We do not want to play into the hands of uncontrolled street and home dealers. The location should not be too close to social places such as schools.

119115.explanation: Everyone should be able to determine how they rent out their property, provided the living spaces meet the minimum standards.

119118.explanation: We see opportunities to combine nature and recreation in a good balance at the Vlagheide. Entrepreneurs should be given enough room to participate without subsidies.

119119.explanation: We are losing the ability to make sovereign decisions about our municipality. Further cooperation with Eindhoven offers no clear benefits for the residents of Meierijstad, while it consumes community resources, time, and money.

119059.explanation: To naturally equalize the balance, the municipality could play a facilitating role in addressing certain obstacles female politicians encounter in their careers.

119060.explanation: The right to speak, but possibly also surveys, participation as a party member or an alternative allocation of municipal funds could also, or even better, contribute to the democratic say of residents.

119063.explanation: I do not know why cafés and restaurants are currently not allowed to set their own opening hours; I would like to first hear the counterarguments before definitively voting on this statement.

119064.explanation: If the energy is for own use or used sustainably, meaning it does not cause further issues with network congestion, then I believe it is beneficial for all residents if more sustainable energy is generated.

119066.explanation: Asylum reception is largely legally mandated and is therefore mainly a national and international issue. The municipality can take limited action without running into fines or legal procedures.

119071.explanation: Although less extreme, the same answer applies here as with the recycling center: charging for bulky waste collection should not encourage people to dump waste in nature. That costs more money and is more harmful to the living environment than free collection.

119073.explanation: I am not aware to what extent this is a current issue; therefore, I will not take a position on this for now.

119074.explanation: It is not clear to me which memberships and sports clubs are involved and what the financial and social consequences of this measure are.

119080.explanation: Ultimately, hiring more municipal enforcers is not a structural but at best a temporary and useful solution to create safety; it is more valuable to invest as a society in removing the causes that lead to crime.

119083.explanation: Equipping enforcement officers with batons can contribute to their safety and effectiveness in performing their work, provided this is accompanied by clear frameworks. At the same time, the focus must remain on addressing the causes that drive residents to crime.

119087.explanation: That somewhat depends on the purpose. If it brings social value, for example through the development of shared housing options, then investors and the municipality can add value. Buying up houses as investment is not intended.

119090.explanation: Forcing people to work when they cannot is absurd; furthermore, one should expect the agency responsible for welfare benefits to grant them only to people who truly need them. If that is not the case, the implementation should be adjusted, period.

119094.explanation: A large portion of the charging points cause complaints because they largely remain empty or were installed without consultation. Assigning parking spaces as charging spots is something the municipality should best coordinate with village and neighborhood councils.

119095.explanation: First of all, without enforcement, a speed limit will not change the driving behavior of most residents. Moreover, a general maximum speed restricts the municipality from providing tailored solutions and choosing where which speed is desirable.

119098.explanation: Ironically, I am religious myself, and one principle of that faith is, to my knowledge, to love everyone. I do not know of any culture, belief, or religion that views this differently, so this statement genuinely surprises me.

119100.explanation: As far as I know, such an initiative already exists within my own community. The experiences have been positive, and it would be desirable to introduce this initiative — where possible — in other communities and neighborhoods so more residents can benefit.

119104.explanation: The municipality should be critical of the types of businesses that establish themselves within our municipality. More business parks are responsible but must demonstrably contribute to the local environment or shape a circular economy.

119106.explanation: As a municipality, you are bound by legal obligations. Status holders, who are legally different from refugees, must be given the opportunity to take steps towards participation in society; detaining them in asylum centers does not solve this.

119107.explanation: The problem created by charging for disposal is that it may encourage people to dump their waste in, for example, natural areas. I am not aware if and to what extent this occurs, but for this reason alone, I would be cautious about charging fees at the recycling center.

119109.explanation: With multiple swimming pools in the municipality, I consider the outdoor pool in Veghel as an additional facility. In times of scarce resources, it is logical that the municipality is cautious, with the success of such an initiative partly depending on local support and involvement.

119111.explanation: Although I do not know the exact annual costs of school swimming lessons, they seem limited, while the provision makes an important contribution to swimming safety, independence, and social development of children, with long-term societal benefits.

119112.explanation: A coffeeshop does not seem to be a problem to me, as long as the municipality thoroughly assesses whether the location is suitable and fits with the future vision of the street or neighborhood concerned.

119114.explanation: More high-rise buildings mean the municipality can develop housing faster and cheaper, while simultaneously saving space that can be used for other social purposes.

119115.explanation: I do not know the arguments against renting out single rooms, but this relaxation seems useful to save space elsewhere given the current housing shortage.

119118.explanation: Nature and recreation can also reinforce each other, as some development can actually create unique habitats for animals. In addition, recreation contributes to financial resources, which are important for nature management and thus the vitality of nature at Vlagheide.

119119.explanation: The initiative does not have to come from Meierijstad. However, it is important to assess whether we can strengthen each other. If there is clear added value for our residents, for example in housing, then it seems advisable to join forces.

119120.explanation: Community funds must be distributed proportionally and fairly across all communities; if one or more communities receive disproportionately more, adjustments must be made.

119122.explanation: Space is becoming increasingly scarce, and the necessity to enable housing construction or invest in an improved living environment has greater social relevance in my view. More conscious use of cars, for example through paid parking, therefore seems a sensible step.

statements.title: Meierijstad should fully commit to cooperating with Brainport Eindhoven.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The residents of Meierijstad should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Meierijstad moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Meierijstad should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Meierijstad should invest more in the development of Schijndel.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality must prevent the opening of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Business parks in Meierijstad should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Meierijstad.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: In the development of the Vlagheide, nature should be given priority over recreation.
Meierijstad should stop funding the outdoor swimming pool in Veghel.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce safety in Meierijstad should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Meierijstad controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality must continue to make funds available for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar blijven stellen  voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Parking in the center of Veghel should remain free.
